_meta.purpose: Permanent regression fixture for the recursive tree resolver. Pins the flat-material totals for four reference blueprints (T1 frigate, T1 battlecruiser, capital carrier, T3 cruiser) that span the algorithm complexity spread. Totals are the MARGINAL (fractional-run) cost basis: an intermediate produced in batches is charged only the fraction of a run a single build consumes (see runsFor in tree-resolver.ts), not a rounded-up whole run.

validation.Rifter: Direct CSV row equality - T1 frigate has no intermediate components, so the resolver output equals industryActivityMaterials rows for (typeID=691, activityID=1) verbatim. Unaffected by the marginal-run fix (no recursion).
validation.Drake: Direct CSV row equality - shallow T1 components produced 1/run, so marginal == whole-run. Unaffected by the fix.
validation.Archon: Deep capital recursion (76 raw materials) through capital/advanced components and batch reactions. Updated for the 3.3.2 CCP-native SDE migration: CCP rebalanced the Archon manufacturing recipe (fewer capital components than the prior Fuzzwork dump), lowering its flat totals; the resolver reshaping was verified correct (Rifter/Drake/Legion and all 62 intermediate sub-recipes matched pre-migration exactly). Operator-signed-off divergence.
validation.Legion: T3 cruiser - deep tree through hull components, Ancient Salvage leaves, and batch fullerene/polymer reactions. The case that exposed the whole-run overbuild bug: at ME0 its buy-side cost is ~186M ISK (matching the operator spreadsheet ~177M within price-snapshot noise), vs ~648M under the old ceilDiv overbuild.
